title: NOC 50011 – Managers – Publishing, Motion Pictures, Broadcasting, and Performing Arts | GTR Immigration
description: Managers – Publishing, Motion Pictures, Broadcasting, and Performing Arts under NOC 50011 are senior-level professionals responsible for leading creative, media, and production organizations across Canada. Classified under TEER 0 (Management Occupations), this role requires advanced industry experience and strong leadership capability. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group50 – Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportSub-Major Group500 – Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportMinor Group5001 – Managers in art, culture, recreation and sportNOC Code50011

duties: Managers – Publishing, Motion Pictures, Broadcasting, and Performing Arts perform some or all of the following duties:
Establish procedures and implement policies in publishing firms, film and video production companies, and broadcasting stations
Plan and maintain production schedules for publications, films, broadcasts, theatre, and video productions
Consult with government regulatory agencies to ensure compliance with broadcasting and cultural regulations
Initiate or approve development of articles, books, films, broadcasts, musical recordings, and theatre productions
Liaise with authors, producers, directors, composers, and creative professionals
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in communications, broadcasting, journalism, or another arts-related discipline is usually required
Several years of experience in publishing, broadcasting, film production, theatre, or performing arts industries are usually required
Leadership and management experience is typically essential
salary_prospects: Managers – Publishing, Motion Pictures, Broadcasting, and Performing Arts typically earn:
$38,900 – $123,300 per year,depending on organization size, province, and industry specialization.
With 8,600 projected job openings, NOC 50011 reflects stable leadership demand in Canada’s evolving media and performing arts industries.
Major employment opportunities are concentrated in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
NOC 50011 is classified under TEER 0 (Management Occupations). Management-level roles are generally eligible under:
Express Entry (if meeting skilled work criteria)
Eligibility depends on education, experience, language proficiency, and program requirements.
Assess your eligibility for Canada PR under management streams
Assist with complete immigration application support

labour_market_demand: Expansion of streaming and online broadcasting
The Canadian media and performing arts sector continues to evolve with technology, creating leadership opportunities for experienced professionals.
Opportunities in major urban cultural hubs
Leadership in film, media, and publishing sectors
Professionals in this NOC play a key role in shaping Canada’s cultural and media landscape.
